tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg: 籊籊竹竿，以釣于淇，豈不爾思，遠莫致之。 泉源在左，淇水在右，女子有行，遠兄弟父母。 淇水在右，泉源在左，巧笑之瑳，佩玉之儺。 淇水滺滺，檜楫松舟，駕言出遊，以寫我憂。

1 With your long and tapering bamboo rods, You angle in the K4e. Do I not think of you ? Bat I am far away, and cannot get to you.
2 The Tsceuen-yuen is on the left, And the waters of the Kce are on the right. But when a young lady goes away, [and is married], She leaves her brothers and parents.
3 The waters of the K^e are on the right-, And the Ts^uen-yuen is on the left. How shine the white teeth through the artful smiles ! How the girdle gems move to the measured steps !
4 The waters of the K(e flow smoothly ： There are the oars of cedar Might I but go there in my To dissipate my sorrow ! and the boats of pine, carriage and ramble,
2 There are the leaves of the sparrow-gourd； — There is that lad with the archers thimble at his girdle. Though he carries an archer^ thimble at his girdle, He is not superior to us. How easy and conceited is his manner, With the ends of his girdle hanging down as they do !
